text: Allowing AI to mediate conflict in a family group chat by rewriting heated messages to be more neutral.
prompts: What assumptions are we making about consent when messages are rewritten?
Could this reduce people's ability to handle conflict directly?
Is neutralizing emotion always a good thing?

text: Using AI to remember and send birthday messages to friends you haven't talked to in years.
prompts: Does the gesture still count if you didn't remember on your own?
Could this actually rekindle a faded friendship, or does it feel hollow?

text: Having AI write your apology text after a fight with your partner.
prompts: Is a well-crafted apology more important than a genuine one?
What does it mean if the AI apologizes better than you can?
Does your partner deserve to know it wasn't written by you?

text: Letting AI suggest conversation topics when you're on a first date.
prompts: Does using a crutch early on set a pattern for the relationship?
Is awkward silence actually valuable in getting to know someone?

text: Using AI to grade and give feedback on your child's creative writing assignment.
prompts: Can AI meaningfully evaluate creativity?
What message does it send to a child when a machine judges their self-expression?
How does this change the teacher-student relationship?

text: Having AI listen to a therapy session and suggest what the therapist should say next.
prompts: Does the presence of AI change how honest the patient would be?
Should therapeutic intuition be augmented or replaced?
Who is responsible if the AI's suggestion causes harm?

text: Using AI to detect when a friend's texting patterns suggest they might be going through a hard time.
prompts: Is this care or surveillance?
Would you want your friends monitoring your messages this way?
What happens if the AI is wrong and you intervene unnecessarily?

text: Letting AI plan every detail of a surprise birthday party for your best friend.
prompts: Does the thought still count if AI did the thinking?
What personal touches might only a human know to include?

text: Using AI to translate your grandparent's native language in real-time during family dinners.
prompts: Does real-time translation preserve or replace the motivation to learn their language?
What nuances of culture and humor might get lost?
Could this actually bring generations closer together?

text: Having AI monitor your teenager's social media to flag potential bullying.
prompts: Where is the line between protection and invasion of privacy?
How might this affect the trust between parent and child?
What if the teen finds out and stops being authentic online?

text: Letting AI write a eulogy for a loved one's funeral.
prompts: Is struggling to find the words part of the grieving process?
Could AI capture what made this person unique to you?
How would attendees feel if they knew?

text: Using AI to coach you through difficult conversations with your boss.
prompts: Does this empower you or create a dependency?
What happens when you need to have a tough conversation without AI available?
Is there value in learning to navigate power dynamics on your own?

text: Having AI generate personalized bedtime stories for your child every night.
prompts: What role does a parent's voice and improvisation play in bonding?
Could AI stories be more engaging than a tired parent's retelling?
Does this change what bedtime means for the family?

text: Letting AI decide which friendships to prioritize based on your interaction history.
prompts: Can data capture the full value of a friendship?
What about friends who matter deeply but you rarely see?
Should efficiency apply to human relationships?

text: Using AI to compose a love letter to your partner on your anniversary.
prompts: Is it the words that matter or the effort behind them?
Would your partner feel differently if they knew?
Can AI understand what your relationship means to you?

text: Having AI provide real-time feedback on your body language during a job interview.
prompts: Does this level the playing field or create a new kind of inequality?
Are we training people to perform rather than be authentic?
What happens to neurodivergent candidates in this system?

text: Letting AI summarize what you missed in a group chat with 500+ unread messages.
prompts: Is catching up on the gist the same as being part of the conversation?
What small moments or inside jokes might the AI skip?

text: Using AI to match students with study partners based on personality and learning style.
prompts: Is there value in learning to work with people who are different from you?
Could algorithmic matching create bubbles in education?
What if the AI is better at this than the teacher?

text: Having AI remind you to check in on a friend who recently lost a family member.
prompts: Does a prompted check-in feel less genuine?
Is remembering to care part of what makes us good friends?
Could this help people who want to show up but struggle with follow-through?

text: Letting AI write thank-you notes to your wedding guests based on what gift they gave.
prompts: Does personalization by AI count as personal?
Would guests notice or care?
Is the tradition of thank-you notes about gratitude or obligation?

text: Using AI to predict which of your relationships are most likely to fade and suggesting ways to maintain them.
prompts: Should we fight the natural ebb and flow of relationships?
Could knowing a friendship is fading become a self-fulfilling prophecy?
Is it healthier to let some relationships go?

text: Having AI facilitate a classroom discussion on a controversial topic by calling on students and summarizing viewpoints.
prompts: Can AI be truly neutral on controversial topics?
What does a teacher bring to facilitation that AI cannot?
Could students feel safer sharing with an AI moderator?

text: Letting AI compose a breakup text that is honest but kind.
prompts: Does the other person deserve a human-written ending?
Could a well-worded breakup actually reduce harm?
Is discomfort with hard conversations something we should outsource?

text: Using AI to analyze your child's drawings and flag potential emotional concerns.
prompts: How accurate can AI really be at interpreting children's art?
Could false positives create unnecessary anxiety for parents?
What happens to the joy of a child's drawing when it becomes data?

text: Having AI create a personalized grief support plan after losing someone close to you.
prompts: Can AI understand the weight of individual grief?
Could structure and guidance help when you're too overwhelmed to think?
What role should human connection play in grief that AI cannot fill?

text: Letting AI rewrite your resume to highlight only what a specific employer wants to see, even if it changes your story.
prompts: Is this strategic or deceptive?
What happens when everyone's resume is AI-optimized?
Does the employer get to know the real you?

text: Using AI to settle an argument between siblings by analyzing both sides and declaring a winner.
prompts: Is fairness the same as being right?
What do children lose when they don't learn to resolve conflicts themselves?
Could this actually reduce family tension?

text: Having AI tutor your child in math using a teaching style personalized to how they learn best.
prompts: Is infinite patience from a machine better than imperfect patience from a human?
What social and emotional skills come from learning with a teacher?
Could this widen or narrow the achievement gap?

text: Letting AI auto-respond to texts from people you don't want to talk to.
prompts: Is it more respectful to auto-respond or to be honest about your boundaries?
What if the other person finds out?
Does avoidance solve the underlying issue?

text: Using AI to generate a weekly family newsletter summarizing what everyone's been up to.
prompts: Does a curated summary replace the messiness of real catch-ups?
Could this help families who are bad at staying in touch?
Who decides what's worth including?

text: Having AI screen your dating app matches and pre-write opening messages for you.
prompts: If both people are using AI, who is actually connecting?
Does this make dating more efficient or more hollow?
What happens when you meet in person and the AI isn't there?

text: Letting AI create a personalized mental health check-in every morning based on your sleep and activity data.
prompts: Could this catch warning signs earlier than you would yourself?
Is there a risk of becoming overly dependent on external validation of your mood?
What if the AI says you're fine but you don't feel fine?

text: Using AI to write college recommendation letters for students a teacher barely knows.
prompts: Is a well-written AI letter better than a generic human one?
What does this say about the recommendation system itself?
Does the student deserve to know how their letter was written?

text: Having AI detect sarcasm and tone in text messages and add helpful annotations for the reader.
prompts: Would this help or hurt people who communicate differently?
Is misreading tone part of what makes human communication human?
Could this be especially helpful for neurodivergent people?

text: Letting AI plan your entire social calendar by analyzing which events would maximize your happiness.
prompts: Can happiness be optimized?
What about obligations that don't make you happy but matter to others?
Would you trust an algorithm with your free time?

text: Using AI to give real-time parenting advice through an earpiece while you're talking to your child.
prompts: Does this make you a better parent or a less present one?
What if the AI's advice conflicts with your instincts?
Could this undermine your confidence as a parent over time?

text: Having AI generate discussion questions for a book club based on each member's reading history and interests.
prompts: Does tailored discussion lead to deeper or narrower conversation?
Is part of book club magic the unexpected tangents?
Could this help quieter members engage more?

text: Letting AI write condolence messages on your behalf when someone in your network experiences a loss.
prompts: Is a heartfelt-sounding AI message better than saying nothing?
Does grief require authentic human presence?
What if your imperfect words would have meant more?

text: Using AI to track and score the emotional labor in your relationship and suggest when it's unbalanced.
prompts: Can emotional labor be quantified accurately?
Would seeing a score help or hurt the relationship?
Is this a tool for communication or ammunition for arguments?

text: Having AI create a digital twin of a deceased loved one that you can talk to.
prompts: Does this help with grief or prevent you from processing it?
What would the deceased person think about being replicated?
Where is the line between memory and simulation?

text: Letting AI suggest when to end a conversation because it's detected rising tension.
prompts: Is avoiding tension always the right move?
Could some difficult conversations be worth having through the discomfort?
Would you trust AI to read the room better than you?

text: Using AI to help a non-verbal student communicate their needs and feelings to classmates.
prompts: Could this open doors that were previously closed?
Does the AI's voice become the student's voice, and is that okay?
How do we ensure the student's authentic self comes through?

text: Having AI compose your vows for your wedding based on your relationship history.
prompts: Is vulnerability part of what makes vows meaningful?
Could AI capture what you can't put into words?
Would you tell your partner?

text: Letting AI choose the perfect emoji reactions to your friend's messages so you seem more engaged.
prompts: If your reactions aren't yours, are you really in the conversation?
Is this any different from being on autopilot while texting?
What does 'seeming engaged' say about the friendship?

text: Using AI to anonymously report a colleague's concerning behavior to HR on your behalf.
prompts: Does anonymity through AI make it easier to report genuine concerns?
Could this be misused for workplace conflicts?
Is there a responsibility to stand behind serious accusations?

text: Having AI create a compatibility score for potential roommates based on lifestyle data.
prompts: Can data predict who you'll enjoy living with?
What about the intangible chemistry of getting along?
Could this reduce conflict or just delay it?

text: Letting AI generate a forgiveness letter to someone who hurt you, even if you never send it.
prompts: Can AI help you process emotions you struggle to articulate?
Is the healing in writing or in the words themselves?
Does it matter if the words are yours?

text: Using AI to help elderly patients articulate their symptoms more clearly to doctors.
prompts: Could this reduce misdiagnosis and improve care?
Does it change the doctor-patient relationship?
What happens when the AI interprets symptoms differently than the patient intended?

text: Having AI auto-generate encouraging comments on your student's work so you can grade faster.
prompts: Does encouragement matter if it's not genuine?
Could students tell the difference?
Is teacher burnout a valid reason to delegate emotional labor to AI?

text: Letting AI read your child a bedtime story using the voice of a grandparent who lives far away.
prompts: Does hearing a familiar voice bridge the distance or highlight it?
How might the grandparent feel about being voice-cloned?
Could this be a beautiful use of technology or a strange substitute?
